seoTitle: Smithfield vs Smoky Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Smithfield and Smoky Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Smithfield vs Smoky Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Smithfield and Smoky Bay.
heading: Smithfield vs Smoky Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Smithfield, South Australia and Smoky Bay, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Smoky Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Smithfield (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Smithfield or Smoky Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Smithfield has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Smoky Bay scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Smithfield scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Smoky Bay scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Smithfield or Smoky Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Smoky Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
